name: Unreal AI Landscape
description: An AI Game Developer (Unreal-MCP) extension contributing MCP tools an AI agent can call against the Unreal Editor.

tools.name: landscape-list-actors
tools.description: Lists every Landscape actor / proxy in the active editor world (read-only), reporting class, whether it is the primary ALandscape (vs a streaming proxy), and component count.

tools.name: landscape-get-actor
tools.description: Inspects a single Landscape actor by name (read-only): section layout (componentSizeQuads / subsectionSizeQuads / numSubsections), component count, landscape material, and paint layers.

tools.name: landscape-list-water-bodies
tools.description: Lists every Water body actor in the active editor world (read-only), reporting its type (River / Lake / Ocean / Custom) and spline-point count.

tools.name: landscape-get-water-body
tools.description: Inspects a single Water body by name (read-only): type, spline-point count, water material, and owning water zone.

tools.name: landscape-list-water-zones
tools.description: Lists every Water zone actor in the active editor world (read-only), reporting world location and 2D zone extent.

_comment: Install-catalog / compatibility manifest, shaped as ONE unreal-mcp-cli ExtensionDescriptor (cli/src/utils/extensions-catalog.ts in Unreal-MCP) so it is directly liftable into the shared extensions.catalog.json { schemaVersion, extensions[] }. 'version' mirrors the .uplugin VersionName (kept in sync by commands/bump-version.ps1). 'minCoreVersion' is the lowest Unreal-MCP core version this extension supports (refreshed by commands/update-core.ps1). 'enginePlugins' are the gating engine plugins (e.g. ['Niagara']) enabled in the .uproject alongside this extension — populated by commands/init -FeaturePlugin. 'tools' lists the contributed tool ids/descriptions. The install-extension resolver unions enginePlugins with the materialized .uplugin Plugins[].
